concerns.text: FR-005 mandates a PAIRED t-test/Wilcoxon comparing LLM vs Human emissions per prompt. Task T032 implements a 'paired t-test or Wilcoxon' but the Plan.md Summary and Constitution Check explicitly specify a 'One-Sample T-Test' (comparing against a constant). The task description lacks the explicit instruction to join LLM and Human data per prompt_id for a paired difference calculation, risking implementation of the wrong statistical test (one-sample vs paired).

FR-006 requires repeating the ENTIRE analysis pipeline (including the statistical comparison) for DistilGPT-2. Task T034 states 'Run entire inference and analysis pipeline' but does not explicitly mandate re-executing the statistical comparison step (T032/T033) on the new DistilGPT-2 dataset. This ambiguity risks a partial implementation where only inference is repeated, failing the robustness check requirement.

FR-007 explicitly requires the report to include 'confidence intervals'. Task T037 lists 'summary statistics' and 'boxplots' but omits the explicit generation of confidence intervals in the report generation logic.

Constitution Principle VII requires every prompt to have both an LLM solution and a human-baseline reference for paired comparison. Task T006 describes synthesizing baseline data but lacks a specific task to validate that the synthesized data keys (prompt_id) exactly match the downloaded CodeXGLUE subset, creating a risk of unpaired data that violates the constitution.

SC-005 requires log output to contain the specific Shapiro-Wilk p-value and the selected test name. Tasks T030-T033 describe implementing the logic but do not explicitly mandate logging these specific diagnostic values to the output artifacts or logs.
